title: Refrigerator Not Cooling
overview: If your refrigerator isn't cooling properly, there are several common causes ranging from simple fixes to part replacements.
diagnosticQuestions: Is the freezer still cold while the fridge section is warm?
Do you hear the compressor running (a low hum from the back)?
Is there heavy frost buildup on the freezer back wall?

followUpQuestions.freezer cold fridge warm: This strongly suggests the evaporator fan motor has failed. The freezer stays cold but cold air can't circulate into the fridge section.
followUpQuestions.heavy frost: Heavy frost on the back freezer wall points to a failed defrost heater. The coils freeze over and block airflow.
followUpQuestions.clicking sound: A clicking sound every few minutes is the classic sign of a failing compressor start relay. It's an easy and inexpensive fix.

diagnosticSteps: Check that the temperature settings are correct: fridge at 37°F, freezer at 0°F.
Make sure nothing is blocking the vents inside the fridge or freezer compartments.
Listen for the evaporator fan — open the freezer and listen for a running fan. If silent, the fan motor may have failed.
Check for heavy frost on the freezer back wall, which indicates a defrost heater issue.
Listen for a clicking sound from the back — this may indicate a failing compressor start relay.

title: Ice Maker Not Working
overview: Ice maker issues are one of the most common refrigerator repairs. The cause is usually the ice maker assembly itself, the water inlet valve, or a frozen water line.
diagnosticQuestions: Is the water dispenser still working?
Can you hear the ice maker trying to cycle (a buzzing or clicking sound)?
Is the ice maker arm in the down position?

followUpQuestions.water dispenser not working: If both the ice maker and water dispenser are not working, the water inlet valve is almost certainly the cause.
followUpQuestions.arm down no ice: If the arm is down and the unit cycles but produces no ice, replace the full ice maker assembly.
followUpQuestions.buzzing sound: A buzzing sound without water filling indicates the water inlet valve solenoid is failing.

diagnosticSteps: Check that the ice maker arm is in the down (ON) position.
Test the water dispenser — if no water comes out, the water inlet valve may be faulty.
Check the water supply line at the back of the fridge for kinks or blockages.
Listen for the ice maker trying to cycle. If it buzzes but no water fills the mold, the valve is the issue.
If the arm is down and water fills but no ice drops, the ice maker assembly has likely failed.

title: Water Dispenser Not Working
overview: Water dispenser issues are typically caused by the water inlet valve, a clogged filter, or a frozen water line.
diagnosticQuestions: When did you last replace the water filter?
Is the ice maker also not working?
Do you get a trickle of water or nothing at all?

followUpQuestions.slow water: A slow trickle usually means a clogged water filter. Replace it first — it's a 5-minute fix.
followUpQuestions.no water at all: Complete loss of water with no filter issue points to the water inlet valve.
followUpQuestions.ice maker also broken: If both are out, the water inlet valve is almost certainly the cause.

diagnosticSteps: Replace the water filter if it hasn't been changed in 6 months — a clogged filter is the #1 cause of slow water.
Check the water supply valve behind the refrigerator is fully open.
If the filter is new and water is still slow, bypass the filter temporarily to test.
If no water at all, the water inlet valve has likely failed.
Check for a frozen water line in the freezer door — use a hair dryer to thaw.

title: Dishwasher Not Draining
overview: Standing water after a cycle is one of the most common dishwasher problems. Usually caused by a clogged filter, blocked drain hose, or failed drain pump.
diagnosticQuestions: How much water is left — a small puddle or several inches?
When did you last clean the filter?
Do you hear the drain pump running at the end of the cycle?

followUpQuestions.filter clean still not draining: If the filter is clean and the hose is clear, the drain pump has likely failed.
followUpQuestions.no pump sound: If you don't hear the pump running at end of cycle, the pump motor has failed.
followUpQuestions.small puddle: A small amount of water (under an inch) is normal — the sump holds a small reserve.

diagnosticSteps: Remove and clean the filter assembly — a clogged filter is the most common cause and takes 5 minutes to fix.
Check the drain hose at the back for kinks or clogs.
Check under the sink where the drain hose connects — ensure it's properly elevated (high loop) to prevent backflow.
Run the garbage disposal if connected — a clogged disposal can back water into the dishwasher.
If the pump runs but water remains, the drain pump may have a blockage or have failed.

title: Dishwasher Not Cleaning Dishes
overview: Poor cleaning is usually caused by clogged spray arms, a dirty filter, or low water temperature. Most fixes are quick and inexpensive.
diagnosticQuestions: Are the top rack dishes clean but bottom rack dirty, or vice versa?
When did you last clean the filter?
Is there gritty residue or food particles on the dishes?

followUpQuestions.top rack dirty: If only the top rack is dirty, the upper spray arm is clogged or broken.
followUpQuestions.bottom rack dirty: If only the bottom rack is dirty, check the lower spray arm and filter.
followUpQuestions.gritty residue: Gritty residue means the filter needs cleaning — food particles are recirculating.

diagnosticSteps: Clean the filter — remove it, rinse under warm water, and use a soft brush to clear the mesh.
Check both spray arms for clogged holes — use a toothpick to clear debris.
Make sure dishes aren't blocking the spray arms from rotating.
Check your water temperature — it should be at least 120°F for effective cleaning.
Use a dishwasher cleaner (like Affresh) to remove internal buildup.

title: Dishwasher Leaking
overview: Dishwasher leaks typically come from the door gasket, a loose hose connection, or the door not latching properly.
diagnosticQuestions: Where is the water coming from — from under the door or underneath the unit?
Does it leak only during certain cycles?
Is the door latch clicking shut firmly?

followUpQuestions.leaks from door: A leak from the door edge almost always means the door gasket needs replacing.
followUpQuestions.leaks from bottom: A leak from underneath usually means a loose or cracked water inlet hose or pump seal.
followUpQuestions.door not latching: A door that doesn't click shut means the door latch assembly needs replacing — the unit won't start properly either.

diagnosticSteps: Check the door gasket (seal around the door) for cracks, tears, or gaps.
Perform the dollar bill test: close the door on a dollar bill and try to pull it out. If it slides easily, the seal is worn.
Check that the door latch is closing firmly and the door is level.
Check under the dishwasher for loose or cracked hose connections.
Ensure you're using the correct amount of dishwasher detergent — too much causes excessive suds and leaks.

title: Dishwasher Won't Start
overview: A dishwasher that won't start is usually caused by a door latch issue, thermal fuse, or control board problem.
diagnosticQuestions: Are there any lights or display on the control panel?
Does the door click firmly when closed?
Did it stop working suddenly or gradually?

followUpQuestions.no lights at all: Complete loss of power with no display suggests a blown thermal fuse — it's a small, inexpensive part.
followUpQuestions.lights work won't cycle: If the control panel lights up but cycles won't start, the door latch switch is the most likely cause.
followUpQuestions.stopped suddenly: A sudden complete failure often means the thermal fuse has blown due to overheating.

diagnosticSteps: Check that the door is latching fully — the dishwasher won't start if the door switch isn't engaged.
Check the circuit breaker — reset it if tripped.
Press and hold the Start button for 3 seconds (some models require this).
If there is no power at all (no lights), the thermal fuse may have blown.
If lights work but cycles won't start, the door latch or control board may have failed.
